column1: # Stack manipulation via the core Forth-style words: `dup`, `swap`,
# `2over`. All are stack-only (BarrierPos=0) and live in
# Argument convention (unified §1.4): args[0] is top of stack,
# args[1] is next-deeper, etc. Handler returns are spliced back in
# source order, so [args[0], args[1]] for swap leaves the old top
# at the deeper position and the old second-from-top at the top.

column3: dup works on any value type
swap touches only the top two
dup then drop is a no-op
drop twice on a 2-stack leaves it empty

column1: # over ( a b — a b a )
# rot ( a b c — b c a )

column3: rotates top three; original top goes deepest
deeper values untouched (1 stays at bottom)
drops the second-from-top, keeps the top

column1: # tuck ( a b — b a b )

column3: copies the top under the second

column1: # 2dup ( a b — a b a b )
# 2swap ( a b c d — c d a b )

column3: removes the top two; empty stack

column1: # 2over ( a b c d — a b c d a b )

column3: copies the second pair onto the top
swap → stack=[2,1] (1 on top); drop removes 1 → stack=[2]
rotate, then drop the new top
